judge_reason: Both descriptions agree on the two photographs, their content (man with glasses, seven bright oval lights with caption), and the quoted caption text, though B adds positional and size details A omits.
original_image_tags: Black-and-white photograph of a man wearing glasses, looking intently through a large telescope or similar optical instrument indoors.

Black-and-white photograph of a series of bright, blurry, elliptical lights arranged in a curved line across a dark night sky. Caption: "These are the Lubbock Lights, as photographed Aug. 30, 1951, over Lubbock, Texas, by 18-year-old Carl Hart, Jr."
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: A scanned magazine page titled “FLYING SAUCERS continued” containing two black-and-white photographs: a small inset portrait of a man holding laboratory equipment at upper left, and a large photograph at upper right showing a curved chain of bright lights across a dark sky, captioned “These are the Lubbock Lights, as photographed Aug. 30, 1951, over Lubbock, Texas, by 18-year-old Carl Hart, Jr.”*
